TextENGNew: The world around me had already been swallowed by total darkness.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "I'm exhausted..."
I had finally managed to escape from the banquet -- or rather, from the assembly hall that had descended into chaos in every sense of the word -- and collapsed onto the shrine's stone steps.
The cool chill of the stone seeped through my clothes, bringing me back to my senses. I let out a long, relieved sigh.
Rena, Satoko, and all the other kids had already made their ruckus and gone home long ago.
Only Mion, myself, and the officially sanctioned(?) chaperone Shion had been kept behind, dragged along by the drunken adults and turned into their prime entertainment.
changeReason: Translation differs significantly in the amount of detail conveyed.

TextENGNew: Mion had been mortified at first, on the verge of tears, but whether she'd snapped or simply given up, she eventually started making the rounds pouring drinks for the adults, firing off one bold witticism after another.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...It looked like she might be pouring oil on the fire, but if I'd gone over to stop her, the situation would probably have only gotten worse.
...That said, acting all embarrassed and reluctant would only make things harder for Mion, so I gradually resolved to roll with it too.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: The plan was simple: let everyone sweep us up in the madness for today, and go back to normal tomorrow.
As for what comes after that... let's not think about that right now.
"Still... Mion's mom was really something else..."

I'd only heard vague stories about her, but she was the textbook case of 'like mother, like daughter.' ...Or rather, like daughter, like mother. In any case, the word that described her best was 'formidable.'
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: I had always assumed Mion's tough, big-sister temperament came from her father, or from her grandmother Oryou -- the family head who was apparently called the 'Sonozaki Empress.' ...But now I knew the truth.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: It was unmistakably from her mother.
When I quietly confirmed this with her father, he gave me a deeply knowing nod and an indescribable look, followed by: 'You too, hang in there~!'
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: A shudder ran through me at the thought that this man might be a preview of my own future.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: "...But they were... good people."

Mion's mother had jumped into conversations with sharp jabs at every turn, while her father provoked laughter with behavior utterly unbefitting a yakuza boss. But it was easy to see just how close the two of them were.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: And I understood then that it was precisely because she was raised by these two that Mion had grown into such a spirited, good-hearted person.
They told me all sorts of stories.
Funny old tales. Dubious ghost stories and folk legends.
And... the dark, sorrowful past of Hinamizawa.
"...The dam... project?"
"That's right.@k@vS14/16/212200006. ...This village, Hinamizawa, was supposed to be sunk beneath a reservoir -- part of some national development plan dreamed up by government bigwigs."
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: "...@k@vS14/01/210100326. Oh, but it was cancelled, right?"

"Aahahaha!@k@vS14/16/212200008. If it hadn't been cancelled, we wouldn't be having this feast right now.@k@vS14/16/212200009. We got it cancelled -- the whole village banding together!@k@vS14/16/212200010. So every moment we're enjoying tonight is a prize we fought for and won."
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: With a spirited laugh, Mion's mother raised her cup and drained it in one go. She must have had a considerable amount by now, and yet she showed not the slightest sign of losing her composure. ...What an incredible woman.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: The anti-dam movement, which had continued until just five years ago.
According to her, the villagers had united to stage protests at government offices and construction sites, drawing coverage from newspapers and magazines. It had been an enormous struggle.
Mion's mother recounted those events in a cheerful, upbeat tone. She was clearly proud of it, but more than that, she had a gift for making the listener feel included in the story.
changeReason: Old says 'it was likely done to avoid alarming the listener'; new says 'she had a gift for making the listener feel included in the story'--different characterizations of the mother's intent.

TextENGNew: That was probably why even those difficult events sounded somehow enjoyable.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: "At first, it was relatively peaceful... petitioning through the legislature, appealing directly to the minister..."
"But the government gradually started throwing its weight around, taking an aggressive stance.@k@vS14/16/212200013. Riot police and all that -- it was a real battle."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "So we fought back.@k@vS14/16/212200015. All of us... including that girl."
"...Even Mion?"

"Oh yes.@k@vS14/16/212200017. That girl had plenty of drive, but she was also clever in all sorts of devious ways.@k@vS14/16/212200018. She pulled off some tricks that no adult would dare try. Gave us a real headache sometimes."
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: "...Hey.@k@vS14/00/mionF10002. There's no need to get into all that..."
Mion's father, who had been sitting beside her, cut in.
"That may be so, but if this boy's going to be Mion's husband, shouldn't he know these things?"
"That's for Mion to tell him herself. It's not our place."
"Fine, fine. I just hate keeping secrets, you know...@k@vS14/16/212200021. Oh well."
Needing to collect myself, I reached for the sake bottle on the tray in front of me. When I turned back, Mion's mother had dropped her smile and was looking at me with a steady, penetrating gaze.
"...You're wondering why I told you all this,@k@vS14/16/212200023. aren't you?"
A natural question. She took the sake bottle from my hand, poured herself a cup, and knocked it back. Then she spoke, choosing each word with care.
"Hinamizawa, you see... has always been plagued by baseless rumors, and people from this village have faced discrimination because of them."
"So saying the community is tightly knit sounds nice, but the flip side is that it's extremely insular.@k@vS14/16/212200026. Outsiders have always had a hard time being accepted."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "So... everyone was a bit worried.@k@vS14/16/212200028. Whether newcomers like you and the Maebara family would be able to fit into the village."
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: "Not at all.@k@vS14/01/210100332. ...Every day is so fun here. I honestly feel glad from the bottom of my heart that we moved to Hinamizawa."
Those words came out naturally, without a moment's hesitation. Because they were my honest, heartfelt feelings.
"That makes me happy to hear."

``That girl... Mion was always concerned about you and Rena-chan. And also the Houjou girl, Satoko-chan.@k@vS14/16/212200032. So, I think that's why she kept the rumors and dark past of this village secret.``
changeReason: Old says 'I think that's why she kept the rumors and dark past of this village secret'; new says 'Wondering whether you were settling in well, whether you were enjoying yourselves'--completely different continuation of the thought.

TextENGNew: "All she wanted... was for everyone to live brightly and happily."
...Come to think of it, Rena had said the same thing.

When she'd moved here last year, Mion's thoughtfulness had helped her blend into the village without difficulty. Mion might seem rough around the edges at a glance, but she was extraordinarily perceptive when it came to other people's feelings.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: "It's a small village, so you would have heard the stories eventually.@k@vS14/16/212200035. But the reason Mion kept quiet about it was because she didn't want you to feel uncomfortable."
changeReason: New translation adds reference to mion, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "So... no matter what happens, I just want you and your friends to believe in that girl's kindness. Can you do that...?"
Behind those words, I could feel a fierce, warm love.
"That girl... has always lived as the next head of the Sonozaki family, one of Hinamizawa's Three Families.@k@vS14/16/212200038. Carrying all the responsibilities and expectations that come with it."
changeReason: Old says 'the next head of the Sonozaki family. And she will continue to do so in the future'; new says 'Carrying all the responsibilities and expectations that come with it'--different concluding detail.

TextENGNew: "...Which is why she has this tendency to unconsciously suppress her own feelings and emotions."
"...Poor thing.@k@vS14/16/212200041. Because of that, she sometimes comes across as cold, or as if she can't read people's hearts... There are those who misunderstand her like that."
changeReason: Old says 'she can come across as a cold, terrible person unable to read other people'; new adds 'There are those who misunderstand her like that'--new frames it as others' misunderstanding.

TextENGNew: "...That can't be..."
Mion's heart is cold...?
No. That was the one thing I could deny with certainty. Someone who thinks so desperately about those around her, to the point of setting herself aside -- how could anyone call that person cold?
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: The entrance test that had led to me becoming class rep was proof of that.
Even if it meant making herself the villain, she would sacrifice anything if she thought it was for someone else's sake.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: A generosity that vast might sometimes look cold, but... I didn't think I was so dense as to miss it. And that wasn't something I would just let slide, either.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "Honestly... there were times I came close to doubting her.@k@vS14/01/210100335. But Mion is the best friend I could ask for. That's the truth."
"...Thank you.@k@vS14/16/212200043. But that's exactly what being everyone's representative means."
"It's a painful position -- sacrificing your own feelings for the sake of everyone else's well-being and stability.@k@vS14/16/212200045. And above all, it's a very lonely one."
changeReason: Old says 'everyone in the village knows that...they couldn't do anything about it. Including me'; new says 'And above all, it's a very lonely one'--completely different concluding thought about the position's burden.

TextENGNew: "...That's why I was honestly surprised when she put forward your name and said she was handing the class rep position to you."
"You might call it just a school title.@k@vS14/16/212200050. Even so, that girl knows the pain of leadership better than anyone."
"Rather than casually dump that burden on someone else, she'd normally just keep carrying it in silence.@k@vS14/16/212200052. For her to entrust it to you... means something."
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: I hadn't been listening to Mion's words with that kind of depth until now.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: I had assumed her excuse of 'studying for exams' was just a cover for her embarrassment... but the adults around her had seen it differently.
Even so.
Oddly enough, my feelings didn't change at all. My desire to stand at the front and lead everyone was no lie, and I had no intention of letting Mion's deep trust go to waste.
"...Oh, but if that's the case... then why tell me all this now?"
Why tell me about Mion's past, and about the real 'Mion'?
I could say with certainty that it wouldn't change how I felt, but... what if hearing this had given me a negative impression of Mion? That could have backfired.
changeReason: New translation adds reference to mion, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: In response to my question, Mion's mother chuckled, raised her cup, and answered.
"Didn't I tell you?@k@vS14/16/212200054. Mion is our precious treasure.@k@vS14/16/212200055. So we have to make sure the person standing beside her is someone we can trust."
changeReason: Old says 'I want her to have precious friends who can help her even more than we can'; new says 'we have to make sure the person standing beside her is someone we can trust'--different request (friendship vs vetting trustworthiness).

TextENGNew: And then.
She fixed me with a sharp, piercing gaze. I almost flinched for a moment, but I met the intensity of her feelings and held my ground.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to mion, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "Honestly, marriage doesn't really matter.@k@vS14/16/212200057. ...Though it'd be nice if it happened."

"But what that girl needs more than anything is friends she can be herself around.@k@vS14/16/212200059. People who'll stand by her when things get rough, and share in her happiness when times are good."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "...And I have a feeling -- purely my own intuition -- that you and your friends can be those people for her."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "I know it's a heavy load to place on your shoulders,@k@vS14/16/212200062. ...but please don't betray that girl's hopes and feelings. I'm counting on you."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "...I will."
The answer left my mouth effortlessly. It came so quickly that I worried it might seem flippant, but Mion's mother looked at me as though she had read my thoughts, and broke into a broad, warm smile.
"Mion is a precious friend.@k@vS14/01/210100343. Leave it to me, Mo--"
"Mgh?"
My head was suddenly seized in a grip. Mion's mother wagged her index finger with a 'tsk, tsk, tsk' and gave me a knowing grin.
"Save that for when you actually marry her!@k@vS14/16/212200065. I'll be looking forward to that day, so take your time."
"Oh, and if Mion doesn't cut it, you're free to switch over to me anytime~.@k@vS14/16/212200067. A younger man is always welcome~."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Pfft?!
I spluttered out the oolong tea I'd been drinking and broke into a violent coughing fit.
I would never be a match for this woman in a lifetime. ...Which meant Mion was going to end up the same way?!

An image of me being completely henpecked by Mion flashed through my mind and I shuddered involuntarily. Thanks to that, I couldn't even look Mion in the eye when she came back from the kitchen carrying plates of food.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: "...Haah."

A sigh escaped me. But it wasn't born of wanting to flee from pressure or complications. Rather... it was the kind of sigh that came from gazing at a vast, undefined future and beginning to sketch its outline.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Marriage, or whatever... that was still a long way off for us. And even the rowdy adults surely understood that much. This was a festival -- a chance for everyone, young and old, to celebrate one of life's milestones in their own noisy way.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Still, everyone grows up eventually. The time you get to be an irresponsible kid is shorter than you think.
Mion, Rena, Satoko, Rika-chan, Shion... and of course, me too.
The days when I only had to think about my own future would someday slip away,
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: and I would have no choice but to recognize that my actions, my words, my very thoughts could affect the lives and existence of others, drawing them in whether they wished it or not.
Supporting each other, relying on each other, standing together against hardship instead of running away.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: That was what it meant to grow up -- and what it meant to pass on to someone else the things you had built.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: I felt like I was finally beginning to understand why so many people had gathered here today.

If I listened carefully, I could hear the bell crickets singing. A different atmosphere from the daytime oil cicadas or the evening higurashi -- something serene and contemplative wrapped the quiet shrine grounds.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: It was a strange feeling. ...In the old days, being alone in the dark like this used to make me scared and lonely. I hated feeling so small and pathetic.
But now... my heart was deeply at peace. My life in Tokyo, which shouldn't have been so long ago, felt like it had been nothing more than a bad dream.
I spread my arms wide and gazed up at the sky full of glittering stars.
The binary stars of the Big Dipper were clearly split into two. Back in Tokyo, I could only see the companion star through a telescope.
A change of environment had made certain things visible that weren't before.
...It might sound like a cliche, but before coming to Hinamizawa, I never imagined that adults, or friends my own age, could mean this much to me.
I was glad I came here. I truly believed that from the bottom of my heart.
...And to keep from forgetting the lessons and warnings born of past mistakes, I wanted to be as important to everyone as they were to me.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: The chirping of bell crickets, and the faint sounds of revelry from the assembly hall.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Mixed in with those, I thought I caught a glimpse of a figure in the darkness, deep within the shrine grounds a short distance away.
I stood up and squinted into the gloom.

Beyond the grove of pine trees it was pitch black, without a single light. A cool but forceful wind swept across my cheeks, and whatever I thought I'd seen seemed to dissolve back into the swaying trees and their rustling.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: "......Keiichi."
Faintly, something like a voice... carried on the wind.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: An auditory hallucination? ...No, that voice had definitely called my name.
"Keiichi..."
Again. This time, even clearer than before.
...What should I do? Go toward it, or...?
No -- I'll go.
Suppressing my hesitation and unease, I stepped forward into the darkness -- deeper into the shrine grounds. Once I'd taken that first step, my feet carried me forward on their own.
The place I arrived at... was a high vantage point I'd visited once before, with a sweeping view of the surroundings.
And then --
"...Rika-chan...?"
"...Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500212. Why are you here...?"
Standing there, gazing down over the village... was Rika-chan.
"I'd been wondering when you slipped away...@k@vS14/01/210100351. So this is where you were."
She seemed genuinely startled by my appearance. Her movements were stiffer and more awkward than usual. Which meant... was that voice earlier...?
changeReason: New translation removes reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "...Were you the one calling my name, Rika-chan...?"
"I didn't call you.@k@vS14/05/210500215. ...But I was... thinking I wished you'd come..."
changeReason: Old says 'But I was thinking you would...'; new says 'But I was... thinking I wished you'd come...'--different implication (expected vs wished for).

TextENGNew: With that, Rika-chan mumbled something inaudible to herself, lowered her face, and turned her back to me. Then she lifted her gaze toward the round, luminous moon floating in the sky.
In the stillness, a breeze swept up from below the ridge, and Rika-chan's long black hair danced as though woven through with flecks of light.
Beyond the veil of her hair, I could sense something in her small back and shoulders -- an unusual coldness, hard and frigid as ice. I stood watching her in silence for a while.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "...Guided by fate...? No, impossible.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1042. But still..."
"It's nothing.@k@vS14/05/210500220. ...Good evening, Keiichi."
Then Rika-chan turned to me with her usual 'Nipah~' smile.
The instant she did, the cold atmosphere I had been sensing from her whole body vanished, replaced by the innocent, endearing manner Rena would call 'kyuute,' as Rika-chan looked up at me.
changeReason: New translation adds reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "You... haven't gone home yet?"
"I stayed behind, saying I had something to discuss with Kiichirou.@k@vS14/05/210500222. Rena took Satoko home with her."
Come to think of it, when the kids were playing during the afternoon, I think Satoko mentioned wanting to see Rena's latest collection of treasures.
changeReason: Old says the narrator is sure Satoko 'will be staying over. Given the time'; new omits this deduction.

TextENGNew: "What about you, Rika-chan?"
"I'm staying at Kiichirou's house tonight.@k@vS14/05/210500224. If I'm not the one looking after him, there's no telling what that drunk old man might do."
"Sounds like the roles of guardian and child are reversed."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "Once Kiichirou has had a few drinks, he needs more looking after than anyone. Nipah~."
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: What a hopeless drunk...
"...Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500227. Have you ever seen the village at night from up here?"
"Huh? No, can't say I have.@k@vS14/01/210100361. Whoa..."
I gently moved to the spot Rika-chan had made room for beside her and looked down at the village. The night view spread before me, softly illuminated by modest electric lights, was quietly beautiful.
"...It's beautiful.@k@vS14/01/210100363. And there's something peaceful about this view. Really calming."
"Every single light you can see from here carries a human life within it."
"Ahahaha.@k@vS14/01/210100365. Who knew you were such a poet, Rika-chan?"
"...Mii. Looks can be deceiving -- I can compose haiku, you know.@k@vS14/05/210500230. Even Kiichirou and Oryou say I have quite the talent."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "Wow, really?"
"Really.@k@vS14/05/210500232. Next time, let's go to a poetry gathering and have a competition."
Th-that sounds like a club activity stacked completely against me...
Mion and Shion both seemed like they'd be strong at that kind of thing, and Rena couldn't be underestimated either. Satoko... would probably have Rika-chan backing her up, so they'd be roughly equal.
Meaning the only people I had a shot at beating were... the younger kids?
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ...Wait, I'm the class rep. I can't set my sights that low from the get-go. A leader should always be competing for the top, even if winning is a long shot.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "Exactly.@k@vS14/05/210500234. A leader must constantly earn both respect and victories to match."
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: "Th-that's a tall order...@k@vS14/01/210100368. Wait, Rika-chan, how did you know what I was thinking?"
"...I think you should really break the habit of saying everything you're thinking out loud."
That sharp retort left me sputtering. ...But looking at Rika-chan's face, I found myself smiling naturally.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "...Mii? Is something wrong?"
"No, I just realized I never said it.@k@vS14/01/210100370. Thank you."
Rika-chan looked at me blankly for a moment, but quickly understood what I meant and broke into a warm smile.
"You're welcome. ...Nipah~."
"Ahahaha..."

The 'thank you' was, of course, for the kick-the-can game that decided the class rep. If Rika-chan hadn't switched sides and kicked that can at the critical moment, things would have turned out very differently.
"When you think about it, today's whole banquet happened because of you, Rika-chan."
"...Not really. I didn't do anything special.@k@vS14/05/210500240. Keiichi, you're the one who moved everyone with your own strength."
"That's not true.@k@vS14/01/210100374. ...But today only happened because everyone was here.@k@vS14/01/210100375. If even one person had been missing, it wouldn't have been the same."
changeReason: Old says 'today happened because everyone was there for me. I'm certain of that, and I'll absolutely never forget it'; new says 'If even one person had been missing, it wouldn't have been the same'--different framing (gratitude vs completeness).

TextENGNew: "...Mii. Those are very fine words."
Rika-chan said that with a smile and gave a gentle nod.
"But I'm a little relieved."
"Because it seems like you're feeling better now, Rika-chan."
A startled look crossed Rika-chan's face, and her smile vanished. And then,
"...I'm fine now.@k@vS14/05/210500245. My cold is gone, and I'm full of energy."
"...Rika-chan...?"
A soft, gentle smile spread across her innocent face.
"I'm sorry for worrying you.@k@vS14/05/210500247. But starting tomorrow, I'll be my usual self. Nipah~."
"What's wrong, Keiichi?"
"Oh, nothing..."
With an endearing, friendly manner, Rika-chan tilted her head and peered up at me.

Those dark, round eyes stared straight into mine, and I nearly looked away in embarrassment. But then I caught a flicker deep within those eyes -- something wavering -- and a different feeling stirred in me.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...I'd noticed it for some time now, but something was weighing on Rika-chan.
She had never once put it into words, but whatever it was had been heavy enough to temporarily withdraw from us. That much I could intuit.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: And all of us had been waiting for the day she would feel ready to confide in us.

But... even looking at her expression just now, it didn't seem like that burden had lifted. And yet Rika-chan was still keeping silent about it. Was it that she couldn't trust us enough to confide...?
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Wh-what was that...?
Just now... I definitely heard a voice...?
"R-Rika-chan...?@k@vS14/01/210100383. Did you just say something?"
Rika-chan blinked in confusion and shook her head. I hastily scanned our surroundings, but... naturally, there was no one else around. Only the rustling of wind-tossed trees.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Then... what on earth was that voice just now...?
...Even so.
"...Guide that child."
Those words had opened a small hole in the wall of doubt I'd been holding in my chest.
changeReason: Old says 'pierced a small hole through my tortured chest'; new says 'opened a small hole in the wall of doubt I'd been holding in my chest'--tortured vs doubt are different emotional states.

TextENGNew: "Keiichi...?"
So I turned to face Rika-chan, who was peering at me with a puzzled expression, and spoke from the heart.
"...Hey, Rika-chan.@k@vS14/01/210100386. You don't have to push yourself, okay?"
Rika-chan stared at me, bewildered.
I regretted blurting that out, but... a tightening pain in my chest pushed the words upward, refusing to stay contained.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: "Right now, Rika-chan... or actually, for a while now... you've looked like you're about to cry.@k@vS14/01/210100388. Like you're holding something painful inside all by yourself."
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: "So... don't carry it all alone.@k@vS14/01/210100391. If I can be of help, I want to be.@k@vS14/01/210100392. I know I'm unreliable, and I'm sure there are things I can't handle... but I'll do everything I can."
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: "...Keiichi."
Hearing that, Rika-chan's smile abruptly faded and she lowered her eyes. And then,
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "...Is this your duty as class representative?"
"Wha--"
I was startled by the rare, faintly barbed edge in Rika-chan's voice. She stared back at me as if probing my true intentions, her gaze unwavering.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: "Well? ...Keiichi."
"Rika-chan..."
I was thrown off by the almost interrogative atmosphere she was projecting.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: ...Her expression had hardened, as though I'd touched on something she didn't want addressed. But even so, I wanted to at least convey my own feelings clearly.
"Class rep... maybe that's part of it.@k@vS14/01/210100396. But more than that, Rika-chan, you're our precious friend."

"So if something's troubling you, I want you to share it. And if there's anything I can help with, I want to lend a hand.@k@vS14/01/210100398. Mion and Rena and Satoko all feel the same way. I'm sure of it."
changeReason: New translation adds reference to mion/rena/satoko, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "...Everyone?@k@vS14/05/210500257. Did you actually confirm that with everyone, Keiichi?"
"Well...@k@vS14/01/210100400. But even without asking, we all --"
"Placing your trust in others isn't wrong.@k@vS14/05/210500259. But if that trust is betrayed... what would you do then?"
changeReason: New translation removes reference to keiichi, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "Did something happen, Rika-chan...?"
"I'm the one asking questions, Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500261. Please answer."
...Why was Rika-chan being so sharp and combative today? And her eyes seemed to say that she wouldn't accept anything less than a sincere answer.
So I... organized my thoughts and answered with the utmost sincerity.
"...You're right. Honestly, I don't know.@k@vS14/01/210100403. But I think being paralyzed by the fear of betrayal, never trusting anyone -- that's its own kind of defeat."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "So once I've decided to trust someone, I don't think about betrayal.@k@vS14/01/210100405. ...At the very least, that's how I want to live."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "...I can't be that trusting.@k@vS14/05/210500263. I know the pain of believing only to have that belief go unfulfilled."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Rika-chan looked down as she said that. ...Seeing her face only deepened my concern.

"What's really going on, Rika-chan?@k@vS14/01/210100407. ...If you feel like talking, I'd like to listen.@k@vS14/01/210100408. Even if it's something I can't understand, it might help just to say it."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "...It's... nothing..."
"Come on, Rika-chan.@k@vS14/01/210100410. If you talk about it, you might feel even a little better.@k@vS14/01/210100411. You can say whatever you want to. I'll listen."
changeReason: Old has lengthy dialogue including 'If you talk about it, it might make you feel a little better...I only earned everyone's trust because of you, Rika-chan. So this time, it's my turn'; new is briefer 'If you talk about it, you might feel even a little better. You can say whatever you want to. I'll listen.'--different content about earning trust is omitted.
Old says '...?' (confused); new says '...Really...?' (seeking confirmation)--different emotional response from Rika.

TextENGNew: Rika-chan looked up and glanced at me. I gave a firm nod and waited quietly, facing her.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Rika-chan gently turned her back and took a step toward the cliff's edge. Then she looked up at the great, brilliantly shining moon in the night sky.
"Rika-chan, careful -- don't go too far, it's dangero--"
"...In that case, Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500269. There is something I want to ask you."
Her voice rang clear, like the chime of a bell.
With her back still turned, Rika-chan clasped her hands behind her and spoke.
"If... there really were such a thing as a predetermined fate...@k@vS14/05/210500272. what would you do, Keiichi?"
"A predetermined... fate?"
"If that fate... were to hurt the things you hold dear and take them from you.@k@vS14/05/210500274. ...What would you do, Keiichi?"
What did she mean? Was this a continuation of her earlier poem?
...No, the mood was far too serious for that. Rika-chan, standing with her back to me, was waiting for my answer in deadly earnest.
"Keiichi. ...What would you do?"
"...Isn't it obvious?"
So, I --
"I'd fight it, of course."
Decisive, serious, straight from the heart.
"I'm not sure exactly what you mean by 'precious things'...@k@vS14/01/210100422. But Mion, Rena, Satoko, Rika-chan... everyone here is irreplaceable to me."
changeReason: New translation removes reference to shion, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "So if anything tried to threaten that -- I'd fight it!"
"...Even if it were fate itself?@k@vS14/05/210500277. Even if it were already decided?"
"Obviously!@k@vS14/01/210100425. I don't believe in some future that's been decided from the start!"
"Then I'll change it myself!!@k@vS14/01/210100427. If it's for my own happiness and everyone else's, I'll take on any hardship, any enemy, any fate!"
"...I see.@k@vS14/05/210500279. You really are... such an interesting person..."
From beyond her small back, I heard the sound of a quiet breath.
And when I realized that faint sound had been a murmur, Rika-chan spun around, the moonlight at her back, and fixed me with the most serious expression I had ever seen from her.
"Very soon... the Watanagashi Festival will begin.@k@vS14/05/210500281. And then --"
"And then?"
"That night... Tomitake will be killed, as Oyashiro-sama's curse."
I gasped, the words dying in my throat. Tomitake... Tomitake-san was a photographer I'd only recently met, someone who visited Hinamizawa every year around this time.
That man... was going to be killed... on the night of the Watanagashi Festival?!
"...And at the same time, the nurse Takano will go missing.@k@vS14/05/210500284. ...But it will be staged. In reality, she will already be dead."
"R-Rika-chan...?!"
"And then, a little while later...@k@vS14/05/210500286. I will be killed.@k@vS14/05/210500287. And after that... everyone in Hinamizawa will die."
The content was so extreme I couldn't even interject. And it wasn't just other people's fates she was laying out -- she spoke of her own death with that same unflinching calm.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: For a moment, I thought it was some kind of joke. But there was no trace of Rika-chan's usual smile or cheerfulness. She spoke as though chewing on each and every word.
Was this... a delusion? A prophecy? Or was it what she had been calling... fate?!
"...I don't know why I'm going to be killed.@k@vS14/05/210500289. Nor do I know every detail of what fate awaits everyone after that."
"I don't have a clear picture of everything.@k@vS14/05/210500291. But... it is fact.@k@vS14/05/210500292. Something that has happened -- every single time."
"...Wh-what are you saying...?"
"All of it, predetermined by fate.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1044. I..."
Rika-chan swept her dark hair back and took a step toward me.
Those shining black crystal eyes stared straight into mine.
Without laughter.
Without sorrow or anger.
Just utterly transparent emotion... eyes so still and unshaken that I could see my own reflection in them.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "I have endured this fate hundreds, even thousands of times.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1046. And once again, the same fate is about to begin in this world."
"R-Rika...chan...?"

"For someone who has seen such a harsh reality, hollow reassurances ring empty -- and infuriating.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1048. I've grown tired of meaningless platitudes. Don't you think that's natural?"
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "...So if I've watched a thousand realities where it never worked out, can you truly show me something different?"
A girl stood before me.
Radiating that same cold aura I had felt earlier. So it hadn't been my imagination after all.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Then who... was this girl standing right in front of me...?
"I think this may be the first time I've shown you this side of me.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1051. ...Yes. This is the real Furude Rika."
"I have lived through the time leading up to my own death so many times I've lost count."
"Your face says you don't believe me.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1055. ...But this is no dream.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1056. If you think it is, go right ahead."
A cold sneer formed on Rika-chan's lips. I started to deny it... then stopped. She would see right through a half-hearted denial.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: So I... could only stare at Rika-chan in stunned silence.
"Please answer me, Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1058. What can you possibly show me?"
"...Rika...chan..."
With wide, astonished eyes, Keiichi stared at me.
...Of course he did. If a little girl who was always giggling innocently and acting cutesy with her 'mii, mii' suddenly started spouting outlandish things, anyone would react that way.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ...I had finally said it.
And at such a time, in such a place, my emotions had broken free, and I had let this version of myself be seen.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: He wanted me to confide in him...? He wanted to help...?
How desperately I had longed to hear those words. And how that kindness gave me the courage and hope to face my fate.
But... were those words spoken from a place of true understanding? Without that, the sentiment they carried was as hollow as empty air.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: I wanted to believe in my friends. I wanted to trust them, rely on them, accept their help.
But... if they truly knew the scale of the darkness I carried, and the weight of it, could they still say the same thing? Could they still feel the same way?
What made it worse was that unlike when I'd confided in the club members in the previous world, this time I had no 'evidence' to present.
Despite such uncertainty, if I steeled myself and confessed, only to be betrayed... then I would truly be alone.
...Even so.
Without meaning to... I had already rolled the dice. The die was cast, literally.
...I waited for Keiichi's response.
In this world, unlike any I had experienced before, Keiichi had exceeded my imagination. When I had been on the verge of surrendering to despair, he had given me a faint glimmer of light.

And where the other club members had always chosen to 'watch over' me in silence, waiting for me to speak first, Keiichi had stepped forward -- pushing in, uninvited, like the meddlesome person he was.
changeReason: New translation adds reference to keiichi, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: So... impulsive as it was, I wanted to take this gamble.
If he believed what I said here, if he accepted this 'me' I had kept hidden... then perhaps I could afford to believe, just a little, in a miracle yet to come.
"Please answer me. ...Keiichi."
I held my breath and waited for his response. If Keiichi apologized, or laughed it off... I would give up and smile. 'It was just a joke. Nipah~' -- I would say something like that.
And after that... I would simply accept that this was my fate. Like the boy who cried wolf -- screaming for help only after disaster struck, and losing everything in the end.
"...Rika-chan..."
At last... Keiichi opened his mouth.
"...Sorry."
"...Is that what you thought I'd say?"
I raised my head, which had begun to droop, and looked at Keiichi. He was grinning, with his thumb raised.

"...Honestly, I don't even understand half of what you said.@k@vS14/01/210100442. And I've been saying all this time that I want to help, I want to fight -- but I still can't see a concrete way to do that."
changeReason: New translation removes reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "...That's right.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1065. In the thousands of times I've done this, the number of people who've believed me can be counted on one hand."
"That's understandable.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1067. It'd be stranger to believe a story like this."
"So what?"
"I said, so what?!@k@vS14/01/210100445. What I don't believe in isn't you or your story -- it's a future that someone else decided! I refuse to accept that!"
"And did you really think I was the kind of guy who'd stand by while a friend was suffering?"
"Ah... u-um, what...?"
"Listen, Rika-chan --"
Keiichi placed his hand on my confused shoulder and gave a deep, reassuring nod.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ...His hand was large, and warm... and unbearably gentle.
"Whether that story was true or a dream, either way.@k@vS14/01/210100449. ...The fact that Rika-chan is suffering because of it -- that much I understand perfectly."
changeReason: New translation adds reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "If you didn't want to tell me, I wouldn't have pried. But the fact that you said it with such anger...@k@vS14/01/210100451. That means you really did want someone to hear you. Right?"
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "So talk to me, Rika-chan.@k@vS14/01/210100453. ...If I can't believe something, I'll honestly say so. But I'll never laugh at you. I swear I won't betray your trust."
"Keiichi..."
...Again.
That face, those words -- they made me hope. They gave me just enough courage to take one step forward, pushing back the anxiety and resignation that threatened to swallow me whole.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Would Keiichi truly believe me? And was it really the right choice to tell him everything here...?
Keiichi held his serious expression and waited patiently for me to speak.
Maybe... No, if it's Keiichi, then surely he could...
But... even so.
Anxiety crept in. Even if I relied on his strength, how would it change anything?
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Convince the villagers? Join forces to fight Takano? ...No, the odds of either succeeding were far too low. And this wasn't something the club members alone could handle.
Should I really confess everything right here and now?
I had shown Keiichi my true feelings out of weakness and frustration, but maybe I was just poking a hornet's nest.
...I knew it was too late for second-guessing, but those doubts and anxieties welled up all the same, and the joy that had swelled inside me shrank in an instant.
"And it's not just me. If you talk to Rena and the others, I'm sure they'd help too.@k@vS14/01/VT4Ba_keiichi1003. So let's tell everyone and think about it together!"
Instinctively, I grabbed Keiichi's sleeve and shook my head. He blinked in surprise and tilted his head, puzzled.
"...What's wrong, Rika-chan?"
"Don't... tell the others about this yet."
"Why?@k@vS14/01/VT4Ba_keiichi1004. Mion and the others would absolutely believe you too."
changeReason: New translation removes reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "...Yes. You believed me.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1077. So maybe the others would too. But... I can't take that risk right now."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "But... isn't that all the more reason to talk to everyone?"
"Nothing starts if we just stand still.@k@vS14/01/210100468. If we put our heads together, we're bound to find an answer."
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: "...But what if we don't?"
"Rika-chan..."
"...Yes, I know.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1082. I've become terribly cowardly."
"But... in the previous world, I told everyone the truth.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1084. And because of that, my precious friends were pulled into the fight... and they died."
...Even now, the scene is seared into my eyes.
I had witnessed countless deaths... but that soul-rending spectacle was more than enough to make my newly mustered resolve falter.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ...I had told Hanyuu back then. That next time, we'd succeed.

But... the confidence behind those words had come from knowing Hanyuu was there. From the assurance that even if I failed again, I could always 'try once more.' That bravado had been built on a safety net.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: "It's true -- I already know who the enemy is.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1086. But... in a world like this, with no guarantees, I can't risk pulling everyone in so carelessly."
"Even if my intentions are good, confessing the truth and taking action could lead to danger."
"...And I'm certain this world is my last chance.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1089. So I can't afford to gamble recklessly."
"The 'other me' who gave me this power.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1091. She was always there, no matter how many times I was reborn... but in this world, she's gone."
"Which means that if I fail this time, I may never be able to start over again --"
That's why I'm afraid.
Is it right or wrong to tell Keiichi the enemy's name, right here and now?
Can I tell him?
Maybe I would only be hastening my own death.
...No, what if I was following a worst-case scenario -- one where Keiichi and the others, who should have been able to live happily, were dragged into it...?
"...So the one who's trying to kill you...@k@vS14/01/210100472. ...you still won't tell me who it is?"
I turned my face down and bit my lip. ...The real me was this much of a coward.
Hanyuu wasn't here.
I couldn't start over.

When I thought of that, the anxiety and weakness I had always managed to conceal now paralyzed me. The strength and unity of my friends, which I had believed to be solid, suddenly seemed as fragile as sand about to crumble.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: And even toward Keiichi, who stood right before me offering his hand with such strength and gentleness, I...
Shame and self-loathing surged through my entire body, gnawing at my heart.
But.
The wretched words that slipped unbidden from my lips... were the honest cry of my heart.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "...I'm afraid...@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1094. To believe... right now... I'm too afraid..."
The silence stretched on.
It was perhaps the first time I had ever heard Rika-chan cry out for help.
If we were friends.
If she was a precious friend.
Then there was only one right answer.
"It's all right. I'll lend you my strength.@k@vS14/01/210100475. Trust me."
I could have said that. It would have been the perfect, textbook response.
But instead, I answered honestly. Because that was what Rika-chan deserved -- she had trusted me enough to show me who she really was, even if she hadn't realized it herself.
"To be honest... I don't know what I can do to help you right now."
changeReason: New translation removes reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "My desire to fight for you with everything I have hasn't changed.@k@vS14/01/210100478. I want to protect everyone's happiness. That's real."
"...But even if I ask you to trust me, I understand that you can't do that right away."
Rika-chan stared at me, her expression rigid.
She must be disappointed... But even so, I clenched my fist and pressed on.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "...But listen, Rika-chan.@k@vS14/01/210100481. There's one thing I want to say.@k@vS14/01/210100482. This much, I can promise."
changeReason: New translation omits substantial content present in the original.

TextENGNew: ``Even if you get me involved in what you call fate, I won't resent you, no matter what happens in the future!@k@vS14/01/210100486. At the very least, I can promise you that!!``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: "...Keiichi..."

"Being scared is only natural.@k@vS14/01/210100488. If I were in your shoes, my legs would be shaking too.@k@vS14/01/210100489. And I don't have the right to tell you to be brave when I don't even understand what you've been through."
changeReason: New translation removes reference to rika/keiichi, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "Maybe you can't believe me yet -- that's fine! Just keep watching!@k@vS14/01/210100492. I'll prove to you that we're worth believing in!"
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "And I'll show you!!@k@vS14/01/210100494. Team up with the version of me in this world, and there's no fate we can't overturn!"
"...You're telling me to believe that, even though I've lost every single time?"
"Those weren't us from this world! They're total strangers!@k@vS14/01/210100496. And you said it yourself, Rika-chan -- this world is different from any you've experienced before!"
"Then believe in the potential of this world!!@k@vS14/01/210100498. We won't lose! I guarantee it -- we'll win!!"
My roar echoed across the silent shrine grounds.
It was absurdly naive, laughably presumptuous.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: But... I was serious. Dead serious.
A single gust of wind swept past, cooling my flushed, burning cheeks.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Rika-chan... stared at me in wordless silence for a long while.
"...Hehe."
"Ahahaha..."

Her face suddenly cracked into a smile, then a snort of laughter, and then she was laughing in earnest. Wiping the tears that had formed at the corners of her eyes, her expression softened completely from the hardness of moments before.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: "You're absurdly honest, you know that?@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1100. And an unbelievable fool, on top of it."
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: "...But that's exactly why I wanted to share this secret with you.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1102. Because I thought you could accept me for who I truly am."
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: "...That doesn't feel like a compliment."
"No, I'm glad.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1104. Keiichi, you've always been this way.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1105. That's why... I'm glad."
"So..." Rika-chan continued, looking straight into my eyes.
"Keiichi... will you show me?@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1107. Show me what you've got -- something that makes me believe it's worth hoping again."
Slowly,
Rika-chan gently extended her hand toward me.
Her fingertips, bathed in moonlight, glowed with an ethereal, pale luminescence.
For a moment, I was captivated by their beauty.
"Rika-chan --"
I took the hand she offered. And I spoke.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "...Understood.@k@vS14/01/210100502. In return, when the time comes, promise you'll tell me everything.@k@vS14/01/210100503. When you're ready to trust us... tell us the whole truth."
"...Yes, of course.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1110. Thank you, Keiichi."
Rika-chan smiled gently at my answer.
"For now, just holding my hand like this is enough.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1112. Stay close to me and make sure I don't stray from the path."
changeReason: New translation removes reference to keiichi, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "If I'm about to fall off the stage, leave your seat and catch me.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1114. That's all you need to do."
"...Will you do that for me? Keiichi..."
"...Yeah. I promise."
We stood there holding hands for a long while, bathed in moonlight.
-- That was the beginning of a long battle.
